column1: # This file is the single source of truth for which FAS
# (Federation of American Scientists) Nuclear Notebook indicators
# the Stage 2 adapter reads. The prototype narrows to the 5
# indicators on the canonical "Status of World Nuclear Forces"
# License: free; cite FAS per docs/source-attributions.md.
# Citation: Federation of American Scientists. Nuclear Notebook.
#   - raw_column         : the FAS HTML table column header (the
#   - rating_category     : one of the 8 rating categories from §4
#   - unit               : the unit string surfaced to users
#   - The FAS table uses literal ``"n.a."`` for not-applicable
#     cells and ``"?"`` for unknown. The reader maps both to
#   - The FAS table uses ranges (e.g. ``"100-120"`` for
#     Pakistan). The reader takes the midpoint and preserves the
#     with a footnote ``a`` that links to the table's note
#     block). The reader strips the letter.
#   - The ``"<10"`` sentinel (North Korea total inventory) maps
#     to the integer 10 (the upper bound) per the FAS convention
#   - The consolidated FAS snapshot page is dated 2014-04-30 (per
#     its <meta name="date"> and the page's footer "Current
#     snapshot year as documented in the page's metadata; the
#     snapshot year is recorded in the run manifest as the
#     are updated more frequently but the consolidated table is
#     the canonical FAS-Nuclear-Notebook summary cited by SIPRI.
#   - The Stage 11 confidence engine penalises the temporal-fit
#     gap between the FAS snapshot year and the prototype's
#     target year (2023). This is a known limitation recorded in
#     metadata + manifest; it is not a blocker for the
#     integrity of the data (the snapshot values are the
#     authoritative FAS estimates for that year).
#   - Add a row per additional FAS table column (e.g. deployed
#     vs reserve counts for a future revision).
#   - Update the per-row count assertions in
#     tests/test_ingest_fas.py if the row count changes.
#   - Update the Stage 9 / 10 score modules to consume the new

column8: Country's deployed strategic warheads (per FAS Status of World Nuclear Forces table); higher_is_better=0 because more deployed warheads = more nuclear risk
Country's deployed nonstrategic warheads (per FAS Status of World Nuclear Forces table); n.a. for most non-US states; higher_is_better=0
Country's reserve / nondeployed warheads in central storage (per FAS Status of World Nuclear Forces table); higher_is_better=0
Country's total military stockpile (active + reserve; per FAS Status of World Nuclear Forces table); higher_is_better=0
Country's total nuclear inventory including retired awaiting dismantlement (per FAS Status of World Nuclear Forces table); higher_is_better=0
